pattern: Sorting turns 'the max of a subset' into 'the last element you scan', so each element's contribution as a max can be built from a running total of every smaller subset's min.
hints: Sort the array ascending; then, for a fixed index treated as the group's maximum, every earlier element can be freely included or excluded and only the smallest included one matters.
Keep a running value g that represents the sum, over every non-empty subset of the elements seen so far, of that subset's minimum.

When you move to a new element x, its own contribution is x*x*(x+g) (the singleton case plus every subset where x is the max), and g itself updates to 2*g + x for the next step -- all taken modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: two elements at the minimum legal value
duplicates mixed with one very large value
unsorted input requiring an internal sort
five small values with a repeat

stress.description: large random array near the modulus boundary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
